title: Unable to access /var/log on host
body:
### Issue Description

I'm unable to access the log files in /var/log of the host, in my podman container.

I'm running rootless podman, but the service in the container (Crowdsec) is running as root. As I understood root in the container maps to my user outside the container (uid 1000) 

From the host itself I can access the log files adn my user account is member of the adm group, but in the container I get permission denied

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. add volume via  - /var/log:/var/log:ro 
2. try to read a log file located in /var/log from the container

### Describe the results you received

Permission denied and ls -la shows:
-rw-r-----    1 nobody   nobody     2940399 May 16 15:26 ufw.log
-rw-r-----    1 nobody   nobody     4151577 May 14 00:00 ufw.log.1


### Describe the results you expected

I expected to be able to read the logs inside the container as I am able to read it outside the container and my uid is mapped to the root user inside the container

comments.body: What are the permissions on the host? It looks like "others" have no read access to this dir so only root and the group can read it.

This are the host permissions:
-rw-r-----   1 syslog            adm             2,9M mei 16 15:39 ufw.log
-rw-r-----   1 syslog            adm             4,0M mei 14 00:00 ufw.log.1

I assume you are part of the adm group?
Containers by default will drop all supplementary groups so you will loose access to anything that is not mapped into the userns. You can use `--group-add keep-groups` to work around that but keep in mind that the users/group in the container will still show up as nobody because it is not mapped.
https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-create.1.html#group-add-group-keep-groups
Compose cannot use keep-groups as far as I know, at least I get an error saying it is unable to find that group.
If compose can use annotations you can set `run.oci.keep_original_groups=1` which should result in the same, I convert this to a discussion as this is not a podman bug,
